channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: If you believed in crypto, you would WANT the price to drop so you could buy more. — 6 replies — 4.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you believed in crypto, you would WANT the price to drop so you could buy more.

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-11 03:06 UTC

OP

If you believed in crypto, you would WANT the price to drop so you could buy more.

If you believed in crypto, you wouldn't constantly talk about it's fiat value.

If you believed in crypto, you would discuss its use cases JUST ONCE instead of it's fiat value.

If you believed in crypto, you wouldn't care about timing the market or any of that faggotry. You would treat it like a permanent asset.

Fact: You DON'T believe in crypto. None of you do. This board is just a bagholder ghetto.

Early replies (first 3)

62211946
>>62211929
you are retarded

62211953
>>62211946
Hold that bag anon, your exit liquidity will come along any day.

62212031
>>62211929
>If you believed in crypto, you would WANT the price to drop so you could buy more.
Still waiting for my 1500 us pesos ether.....

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62212204
>>62211929
You are right and that's why crypto is practically dead.
The average person may be somewhat dumb with money, but they are smart enough to avoid well known schemes.
The gamblers stuck with crypto are, more or less, the people who killed it.

62214091
>>62211929
I believed in a crypto project, and I think it has good use case, but yeah most crypto bros are just in it to get rich quick and don't actually care about the actual value of crypto. Honestly they ruined crypto.

62214092
>>62211929
>you would WANT the price to drop so you could buy more
buy more for what?

item.title: Nintendo Criminally Undervalued — 67 replies — 33.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo Criminally Undervalued

Open thread • Replies: 67 • Updated: 2026-05-11 02:20 UTC

OP

Honestly believe Nintendo is grossly undervalued. Considering a buy at current levels. Thing is, I’ve never bought an ADR before. What are the advantages/disadvantages of buying an ADR

Early replies (first 3)

62212154
>>62212149
Not buying that bing bing wahoo slop. Sorry.

62212158
>>62212149
I tried this when it dropped to 16. Stopped out at 12. I would let it bleed for a while might go lower.


About ADRS… beats buying on the tokyo exchange. Its 100 shares minimum on tokyo. Adr you can buy any amount. Only disadvantage is lower liquidity. If you want the actual shares use ibkr for tokyo, otherwise NTDOY is fine.

62212161
>>62212149
Nintendo was trading around 3 dollars during the wii u saga. It can easily drop another 50% from here.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62213496
>>62213330
Nah Nintendo is very different than apple. Nintendo actually innovates, Apple just steals Nintendo's innovations. For example Nintendo made the first commercially successful pocket-sized wi-fi compatible touch screen gaming device. Only after Nintendo cleared the way Apple copied their homework.

62213503
>>62212161
Switch 2 is the best selling game platform of all time, Wii U sold poorly. It's a totally different state of affairs.

62213504
>>62213496
>Apple just steals Nintendo's innovations.
HAHAHAHA holy shit lmao. truthnuke

62213506
>>62213503
fuck the switch man. just download nintendo classics. teach your kids how to get the classics.

62213508
>>62213506
Kill yourself faggot

62213510
>>62212149

62213512
>>62213496
That was 30 years ago. What have the done since?

62213514
>>62213496
That is a TERRIBLE reflection on Nintendo if they can only capture a tiny, tiny % of the value they create.

62213519
> don't tell anyone
GTA VI has picrel for in-game currency

screenshot this and thank me never you dumb fucks

62213520
>>62213512
Their current offering is a handheld that you can connect to your television to play games in 4K with mouse controls, rendering all of the reasons someone would choose PC gaming completely obsolete. Also several of the games they have on offer look better than anything on PC, for example Metroid Prime 4 and Mario Kart World.

62213527
>>62213514
It means Nintendo is not an agent of the system but that the system is actively working to suppress Nintendo's success, and also that the system failed to do so.

62213530
>>62212149
22 pe ratio which is still high.

62213531
>>62213496
>Nintendo is very different than apple.
yeah apple stock was a good investment

62213534
smartphone is something everyone has > toy almost no one has

invest wisely

62213535
>>62213531
I'm literally rich because I preordered a 3DS when they came out and now it's worth thousands of dollars

62213536
>>62213535
lol

62213542
>>62213536
I'm just saying, this shit is a good investment. The value is there. When Nintendo drops a mario or zelda game for Switch 2 people are gonna lose their shit. Keep in mind that when they revealed Tears of the Kingdom, the reveal was only a few months before the release. They are trying to avoid announcing games a long time before they release now. So changes will come quickly and it will be a shock to people when they see how good the graphics on the next Zelda will be, it will make everything on PC look like a joke.

62213549
>>62213534
>Buy the thing that pumped already
>Don't buy the thing with massive potential and good odds but hasn't pumped yet
Yep we're in a /biz/ certified hood classic thread

62213569
>>62213510
>>62213530
Started watching it at $13. Decided to not pull the trigger. In the $10s I’m still hesitant. Probably going to start buying in the mid/low $9 range. If Nintendo plays it’s cards right, I see potential for decent gains

62213570
>>62213542
Metroid had a 78 on Metacritic. Bananza didn't even crack 90. The last Zelda (Echoes) was the lowest-scoring in Nintendo's history of Zeldas. The dev culture has become poisoned by the shitty zoomllenial worth ethic. The old guard is retiring (Tezuka retired yesterday). Women are being promoted recklessly (see the Echoes dev). The upper management seem utterly clueless about all of this.

62213590
>>62213503
Software attachment rate is under 2 so far. Thats bad, maybe the worst for any Nintendo console in 40 years through its first year. This conpany is barely making any money on hardware

62213646
i went to see a palm reader and she told me that in 2030 nintendo will fuse with valve to release their games on PC, effectively ending console gaming and all alternatives to steam.

62213657
>>62213569
I'd buy at the 5 range

62213925
Crashing 12%
LMFAO LOL

62213949
>below LINK
Ohhhhhh Nintendieeeeeeeeeees

62213977
>>62213235
>You are aware that shares can go to zero right?
>"I don't mind it going to zero"
Do you know how to read? More upside doesn't mean stock will bounce up and down. It means the reward ratio of it being 0 is close to impossible due to it being backed by Tencent, having enough equity to pay off the debt, having IP's 4 times worth more than the debt.

>>62213213
>Gamers are so fucking stupid it's unreal. Ubisoft is a dying company, 9/10 of their projects don't make enough money
Wow what thoughtful response. Except it's not 9/10. It's been the last 4 games due to them being complacent with selling 7/10 games which made them a lot of money for a time. The entire company has restructured, they've gotten old game devs, quality controller much stricter. You can easily see it in AC BF Resynced and majority of the game was still made under the old team. Also, Tencent isn't going to let their 800m (and additional 1 billion on IP) dollar investment just go into flames. Ubisoft made a deal with the board (including Tencent) to not be takeover by Tencent for 8 years in 2022 to allow for a turnaround. This deadline is in 4 years. Tencent will want to fully take over the company by 2030 and have every incentive to not allow the debters to take their investment and IP.

62213982
>>62213925
Nintendos Market cap is below Activision when it got bought out for 69Billion. I doubt Nintendo isn't worth 55B.

62214016
Gaming is dead. All new games are something redone and done again, remakes and remasters, nostalgia bait and whatever. A lot of old IP are being killed by woke shit. A lot of people are just buying emulation consoles and playing old games. The last console generation with a huge amount of good games was ps2 (just for example: gta3, gta vc, gta sa). After that, the games that people are playing like crazy are the ones that people are addicted for decades like cs and dota.

62214017
>>62214016
Games that have online multiplayer with micro transactions, that's why on ps2 we had 3 gta, but gta v had 3 playstations.

62214026
>>62212149
Not buying your bing bing wahoo. Simple as

item.title: The LINK token has value — 177 replies — 29.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
The LINK token has value

Open thread • Replies: 177 • Updated: 2026-05-10 22:16 UTC

OP

The LINK token has value

Early replies (first 3)

62176216
New Chainlink report
https://bitwiseinvestments.com/crypto-market-insights/chainlink-in-plain-english

62176226
>>62176214
>In many cases, nodes must stake LINK before they can provide and be compensated for their services. If they provide incorrect data, they are penalized and lose a portion of the LINK they have staked. This system helps ensure the integrity of Chainlink’s services while also
reducing the free-floating supply of LINK.
Speaking in present tense. I assume this and the and the other clues we've seen in the past couple weeks mean staking imminent?

62176244
>>62176226
What other clues

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62206464
>>62206453
notice how the shill instead of providing basic on-chain proof resorts to insults immediately
unhinged

62206529
>>62206464
Who are you talking to bub?

62206867
>>62205875
Holy fuck you people are retarded, mp4's have been available for a while now you just can't use audio on non /gif/ boards. I really can't tell if the people fudding are this stupid or disingenuous. Surely they are all from ebaumsworld.

62206946
>>62206867
kek
>>62206129
>>62206205
>ask questions expect answers
>call everyone who tries to answer a shill
pick one

62206969
>>62206946
billions in value moving to chainlink

62207057
>>62206946
kek it's like he's mad at shills both for shilling, and for not shilling hard enough.

62207097
>>62206529
he demanded truth instead of trust, something chainlink is unable to provide

62207144
>>62176214
>>62176216
Attention all linkies, baggies and fourth industrial resolution shills reading this

Have you no shame, no decency, no morals or soul? If you stay your course and don't repent and turn away from your grievous sins, your souls are consigned to hell in the afterlife for your despicable, disgusting and evil acts in this life.

No one likes you, appreciates you, looks up to you, respects you, or can even stand you. Everyone hates and despises you and has nothing but disdain and contempt for you, even the genocidal tyrants whose eager enforcers and accessories in genocide and crimes against humanity you are. You are despicable evil. You are revolting to all decency and morality. You are a disgrace and a stain on history. You are foot soldiers and cruel attack dogs of murderous despots. Your names and memory will live in infamy. You will be pointed to as examples of the worst dregs of humanity

Linkies will stand trial at Nuremberg 2.0 and be hanged for their heinous complicity and accessory to genocide and crimes against humanity as well as their loyalty to genocidal tyrants.
Everyone alive to witness Nuremberg 2 will cheer your sentencing and rejoice at your execution. No one will come visit your graves, which will be nameless, or lay flowers for you there, not even your spouses, parents or children. They will be ashamed to have been related to you, married to you, descended from you or to have borne your name.

Stop. Think. Reconsider. Repent. Denounce Chainlink and ask for forgiveness for all the evil you have already committed.

Apologize to and ask for forgiveness from the innocent anons you've been oppressing, repressing and persecuting.
Resign or reverse course and help the resistance against the Great Reset which is unfolding while there is still time to make a difference and before you have even more innocent blood on your hands

62207187
>>62207097
Node payments are all on chain, how is that not truth?

Also, how many times have you typed this "truth over trust" line so far in the past years? Couple thousand?

62207249
>duuude just go check it all on the blockchain!!!!

umm ok, where is that

>duuuuuude are you serious, just read insert buzzword blog here

nah i'm good

if all that shit is so important, why doesn't the token go up

>duuude it's up a million % from the year 1896

oh but didn't those gains happen right at the beginning?

>duuuuude you should've bought then

yeah i dont have at time machine

>duuuuude y y yer a fudder!!!!

i just said what the actual price action was

>duuude do you even read the whitepapers??

yeah i skimmed it, it didnt say much. now what

>duuuude we won against layerzero

oh nice congrats
but how does that help the token again

>duuude y y yerrr brown!! (blocks)

62207426
>>62207249
>yeah i skimmed it, it didnt say much. now what
Try again later!
>>62207097
some people choose not to trust no matter how much truth you provide. Chainlink is making products that run in trustless environments for those people too

62207443
>>62207426
zk snarks have become trustless, not requiring trusted setup, but Chainlink says they take too long to setup for cross chain connections

62207489
>>62206946
>shamelessly sneaking in 2 extra zeros like that

62207526
>>62206946
you haven't given any answers
>>62207187
truth over trust is a Chainlink moto, are you schizophrenic?

62207598
>>62207526
your fud could use some work bro
take it from a pro like me

62207606
>>62207598
bro your shitposting might be funny at times, but ultimately it's always shitposting

62207650
https://x.com/LinkBoi777/status/2052815025828516032
WAGMI bros

62207825
>>62207526
>truth over trust is a Chainlink moto
so why are you - who don't care for Chainlink - typing out this motto a dozen times each and every day?

>are you schizophrenic?
Says the obsessive nutcase with at least 5 IDs itt

62207888
>>62207526
And you're the retard accusing people of being in cahoots with mods by posting mp4's when you've been able to do that for a while now, you don't even know how this website works you fucking faggot, so go back to whatever discord you came from and try again next week.

62207984
>Saar, you must understend, no one is shilling link
you reek of curry

62208017
You have so many opportunities to get a bag, but you choose to flame on /biz/ for years like its your job
Everything is true, its been out for years, its coming and you choose to still argue against it, m8
lmao
Im feeling stinky today!

62211139
>>62176214
sorry token not needed

62211186
>>62176214
the whole right side paragraphs are made up bullshit

it should be criminal getting peopel to keep investing in this token,

chainlinj is a cook project, but the token dumps should be criminal ]]stay away from link newfags

62211221
>>62211186

62211383
it has been 128 days since sergey was last seen.

WHAT HAPPENED? I WANT ANSWERS

62211460
>>62211383
developing and accumulating, enjoying privacy

62213093
>>62176214
The problem is that no one actually needs to use chainlink services. Smart contracts just aren't really needed in most industries.

62213123
>>62213093
This. So much this.

62213124
>>62211383
kek that pic

62213442
>>62213093
Like most technology, it takes a competitor implementing their smart contracts system with chainlink oracle inputs that's 100x cheaper and 100x faster before a company scrambles to figure out smart contracts

item.title: EMERGENCY BOBO MEETING — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
EMERGENCY BOBO MEETING

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-11 04:11 UTC

OP

B-bobros why is it not even touching below 80k? We were in the middle of a golden bear not very long ago

Early replies (first 3)

62213614
That was a mid-cycle correction, Bobo.

62213625
>>62213601
Moving sideways in a tight range might be an accumulation. Has Saylor said anything lately?

62213634
>>62213625
The boomer started to realize that the ponzi is over and is floating the idea to defect. Also sideway in nominal terms in real terms its still going down and hasn't gone up for the better part of 7 years

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62213685
>>62213601
Checking in this cant be real

62213732
This pump is horseshit and you losers know it

62214240
BTC holds until the alts are done doing pum n dumps.

item.title: Bitcoin or altcoins? — 80 replies — 11.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin or altcoins?

Open thread • Replies: 80 • Updated: 2026-05-10 21:01 UTC

OP

If you had to pick, what would you buy right now: BTC or altcoins? I’m not seeing much ROI in alts lately, even utility or memecoins.

Early replies (first 3)

62195293
>>62195275
Bitcoin until 120k

62195300
you can't time the market I thought like >>62195293
last time, bitcoin to 70k and sell half and go into alts and then 2021 alts just fucking died for a year.

The alts that pumped are things that haven't gone parabolic yet, or big projects like Solana with glowie money attached to them. Ones like Link are not going to 1000 regardless of cope.

/biz/ will pretty much only give you bad info too. I've found maybe 3 coins here that were Guaranteed returns, everything else I had to research myself. In fact I remember last year posting proof that CRCL stock was going to 10x on IPO and got XRP schizos fudding me out of taking a life changing position.

I actually hate the fuck out of baggies.

62195373
>>62195293
Why 120k only?? Why not 1M?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62208072
I think the days of everything pumping together are over.

62208095
>>62207902
If you can’t handle volatility, just stick with BTC.

62208114
>>62195275
BTC dominance still killing most alt momentum rn.

62208155
>>62195275
Utility coins barely move unless the market gets euphoric again. Wth

62208168
>>62208095
I was asking why he would sell at 120k....

If it reaches ATH again, it's going to go higher.

62209247
>>62205030
Good choice anon. Never really liked the whole wrapped BTC meta either.

62209253
>>62208030
Yeah and still think the space matures eventually once better infrastructure and security become the priority.

62209260
>>62208168
Are you also holding?

62209263
>>62207947
Yeah true, but can’t really generalize everything either. Some protocols got hacked for completely different reasons too.

62209287
>>62208072
days over? You're lying

62209291
>>62209260
Hodl

62209304
>>62195275
BTC dominance still looks too strong for a full alt run right now.

62209376
>>62207965
Let's see how it goes

62209379
>>62204588
So are you into btc?

62209385
Bitcoin is getting fucked

62209525
>>62209385
Utility coins barely move unless the market gets euphoric again. Just btc

62209590
People pretending fundamentals matter in crypto will never stop being funny.

62209854
>>62195275
Everyone becomes a macro analyst after holding one red candle lmao

62210047
>>62209304
but when?

62210050
>>62209291
hold and stake

62211194
>>62207855
I've got some hbar in my portfolio

62211429
>>62211194
hbar going down to 2c before it ever gets a chance at real traction

62211440
>>62195275
>Chainlink is the future

62211870
>>62211440
Hahaha

62211903
>>62195275
Everyone moved to BTC of course, I dumped my ETH for BTC too, I used to hold BTC because of staking but now I can stake BTC natively thanks to TBV and the likes

62211915
>>62195275
I’d rather keep stacking BTC, then put it to work via native staking / yield layers (like Babylon-style setups) instead of chasing dead charts and hoping for a narrative pump.

62211928
>>62209385
Ok as you say so

62211932
>>62211429
Hbar is okay to hold and buy

62212034
>>62195275
More potential upside on alts for the next six months. Specifically memes right now.

62213245
>>62211440
What's your price target anon?

item.title: is zcash superior because it has optional privacy, therefore being able to skim through AML? — 52 replies — 8.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
is zcash superior because it has optional privacy, therefore being able to skim through AML?

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-05-10 22:26 UTC

OP

it has vastly overtaken monero. but it is not "used" as much as monero

Early replies (first 3)

62194143
>>62193936
>it has vastly overtaken monero.
The last time it has overtaken XMR twitter personalities got paid to shill zec.
>therefore being able to skim through AML?
Depends on how the law is written, definitely a grey area for many AML laws.

62194158
>>62193936
Monero is for midwits

62194187
>>62194158
>Monero is for midwits
true.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62203136
>>62203128
OTOH they released the best privacy tech to date by far. Maybe 2.2M is acceptable considering they can't ask for VC funding, have to lay low/stay anonymous and fund themselves somehow? Idk.

What I know is that if I'm looking for privacy, nothing else comes remotely close to Dero tech wise.

62203225
>>62203122
>>62203128
Midwit takes.
Real solution: coinjoin. On Bitcoin.
Also, LN, on Bitcoin.

Sorry, your alt is not needed.

62203313
>>62203225
Sorry, already been evaluated and rejected for sucking.

62203334
>>62203313
>some druggie is afraid of using tech
cool story bro

62203382
>>62203225
>coinjoin and ln
kek le bitshitter cope gtfo nigger

62203402
>>62195101
Druggies are midwits at best. Losers pissing their lives away over plants soaked in acetone.

62203465
>>62203334
>>some druggie is afraid of using tech
More like tech-savvy criminals with everything on the line have found BTC's privacy "solutions" inadequate and unreliable while Monero ticks all the required boxes.

62203635
>>62203465
You would get laughed at so much if you didn't take yourself so seriously. Try leaning into it instead of sneeding over criticism Mr ex XMR general OP

62203740
>>62203635
Non-rebuttal rebuttal.

62203823
>>62203122
sorry what? ZKPs do not cause "inflation risk"

62203853
>>62203740
You've been posting the same shitty takes for the better part of a decade, immune to all arguments. You don't deserve rebuttals at this point, you deserve ridicule.

62203902
>>62203853
Nice try but still a non-rebuttal rebuttal. Because you have no rebuttal. So you try to distract with ad hominems. Keep going.

62204119
>>62203122
>>62203128
>>62203136
>Dero shill

See >>62204099

62204445
>>62203902
My only thing I am arguing is that you're retarded and immune to logic. I reference your long history of being retarded and immune to logic, so that I don't have to slog through another retarded conversation with you. If that's an ad hominem, that's the point. I'm arguing about you, not Monero. Maybe you have trouble distinguishing between the two because it makes up 100% of your personality.

62204821
>>62203402
yes
but the liquidity on DNM is still dependent on those midwits
zcash shielded pool needs a specific wallet with anti-retard infra if it wants to even replace bitcoin on DNMs

62204824
>>62203334
that's about right
state channels have very garbage infra for self custody, most solutions involve routing through custodial as backup
that's too many footguns for DNM guys, you will never see LN adoption on any escrow, save for rare exceptions where two ultra-bitcoin nerds collide

62208887
zcash won. shit isn't going down.

62209857
>>62203122
>Or they can be flat out leaked through non reproducible wallet implementation backdoors and become a master key that completely deanonymizes the network
Regular wallet keys can also be leaked to deanonymize the network you lying fag.

What a load a FUD.

62209871
>>62193936
They are both not used as much as Litecoin with mweb

62209999
>>62209871
deluded

62210510
>>62209999
Wow, very strong argument there. Can't argue with that

62210635
Meme or not wownero is the best privacy coin there is. Too bad tradeogre fucked it. Still buying but it's annoying now

62210649
>>62193936
>it has vastly overtaken monero
lmao retard. Monero was "ahead" of zcash like a month ago, scam pumps don't matter.

62211809
>>62209857
Putting a backdoor to steal a view key is legally different from a backdoor to steal a private key. From a regulatory point of view, you can force devs with special court orders to install a backdoor that leaks view keys because view keys do not take away agency from the wallet owner. In other words, whoever owns that wallet remains as the only person capable of signing transactions with it, and therefore is liable for anything that happens with that wallet (eg: crimes such as ill gotten gains or attempts to launder, or just simple personal financial activity).

So NSA lawyers aren't going to sign off a system that leaks private keys, and if any dev did it that would be a malicious wallet. Malicious wallets can exist both in coins with view keys and without view keys. That's another matter.

But if your coin has a view key, which Monero today has and with FCMP view keys are going to be even more powerful since they will be FULL view keys and therefore make a wallet completely transparent for whoever has that key, then NSA lawyers will want a backdoor to get that view key. Since that view key is only a matter of surveillance powers, and does not destroy agency ("non repudiation").

And by the way, non reproducible builds are just 1 attack vector, they could use another thousand technical loopholes/vulnerabilities to leak them. Knowing how exactly is besides the point, most of us will never know because we don't work for the NSA and don't have a deep knowledge of where the vulnerabilities lie. What we know is that it makes perfect sense legally.

And I wouldn't be surprised if current Monero wallets are already leaking incoming view keys.

62211810
>>62211809
So please, don't be an idiot, don't use a privacy coin that has incoming view keys, or even worse a privacy coin upgrading to full view keys "so you can check your balance while being offline" (the most cringe justification for surveillance I have ever heard). Leaking those keys is a question of surveillance, and surveillance is 100% legal.

OTOH, nobody can put systemic backdoors to get your private keys because it would destroy non-repudiation. But they can and will get your view key if the privacy coin you use generates view keys at the protocol level.

62211825
>>62193936
Not selling Monero. Not buying Zcash. Simple as.

62211836
>>62211809
>From a regulatory point of view, you can force devs with special court orders to install a backdoor that leaks view keys because view keys do not take away agency from the wallet owner.
Which law school did you go to again?

62211862
>>62211809
>So NSA lawyers aren't going to sign off a system that leaks private keys
Yes, after all the NSA is famous for adhering to the law and respecting people's rights.

I remember when intel wanted to implement the Management Engine in their CPUs and NSA told them "No! Don't do it Intel! This will destroy agency! Think of the non-repudiation!" and Intel thought of it and backed down and now we thankfully fully own our computers. Thank god we had those NSA lawyers thinking about our agency amirite.

62211869
>>62210510
>Hey guys look at this graph of payment methods in Germany. Lol USD got rekt, nobody uses it anymore!

Bitpay doesn't even support Monero you stupid fag.

62213468
>>62210510
that's bitpay you fucking mongrel. do you know what heleket is?

item.title: No one is talking about how there's no jobs. — 95 replies — 7.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
No one is talking about how there's no jobs.

Open thread • Replies: 95 • Updated: 2026-05-10 15:23 UTC

OP

No one is talking about how there's no jobs. Ive been working for 10 years and never seen the market nearly this fucked up. Every job is fake, they will interview for 3 or 4 rounds and still repost the job next week, after ghosting people of course. People irl are coping so hard. Im back for my masters and not one person has any job or internship in any of my classes this or previous semester except me(and I will get laid off soon). These zoomers are so fucking deluded. They cant get an internship so they say "of well, im just gonna focus on finishing my masters". Is this how THEY are getting away with it? Society has already brainwashed people so badly the people in charge can drink their milkshake and leave them destitute and they wont even notice?

Early replies (first 3)

62184289
>>62184286
skill issue. ggf &c

62184301
>>62184289
All these interviews just happened to not like me or anyone else they interviewed and reposted the job? What a coincidence. Crazy how i had skills for the past 10 years and always instantly found a job but something randomly happened and now I lost whatever skills I have as I got good excperince in my fields

62184315
>>62184301
Oh and here is the email from my on-site interview on may 4th i got on friday. Nevermind it was my drram company and this tore my fucking hear out. Skill issue on my part that they canceled the role..

Oh and this is only 2/3 of all my interviews this past months if u count phone calls

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62188926
>>62188815
>>62188832
Israeli IP

62188963
you laughed at le trade meme
hahhahahah
I can work anywhere I want, more jobs then I can shake a stick at, getting phone calls about asking if Im available for dispatch, sorry currently employed!

62190507
>>62185702
That all just sounds like bullshit

>>62187055
Huh? If the count was based on people who want a job why are you talking about slavery

62190516
>>62185827
>>62188258
Those are just an obnoxious type of ghost listing
https://www.fastcompany.com/91019872/employers-are-intentionally-leaving-job-openings-unfilled-heres-why

62190529
capitalism doesn't work
king now
kill jew
sell orc inc mik wop feminists
force abourt preg not want
free slave girls n rooms for white male engineer, everyone else 0 money
red lights gone instead put road circle small
highway have middle u turn lanes, no overpass
flibe.com free power
greenarrays.com replaces computing university school lawyer movies games net etc.
more barges n canals
monorails
trains
etc
dymaxion car burning hydrogen in stirling engine exhaust water
dam huge waterway for hydroelectric
100% white west playground :)
king all the way

62192053
>>62188815
kys shill-rat.
>>62188832
agreed, thats why i hate kikes, they invented communism in their kike-think-tanks, weaponised it and unleashed it on Russia+china, and the world. Kikes are either directly, or indirectly, behind 95% of all human suffering for the last ~200 years.

62194013
>>62184286
Everyone is talking about that - on plebbit and normie sites. This board is for retards with $3000 saved to gamble it on shitcoins dreaming of becoming a millionaire.

62194027
>>62185045
>network engineer
>CompTIA
Doubt. Even a CCNA won't get you a job.
CompTIA is basically worthless.

62194055
>>62184286
>Ive been working for 10 years

Woah, woah, woah Hold up there gramps.

I haven't seen attack ships on fire off the shoulder of Orion, but I am getting video interviews at least. The problem is usually the company will cuck out at the last minute and cancel the req before I get a chance to start. I've been between jobs now for 8 months, almost 10% of your career, and it happens quite a bit. I suggest you pick up a book. Maybe pass the 3 CFA exams. Until everyone learns to stop being faggots about AI, we're in for a bumpy ride because galaxy brains like yourself think you can automate and forget it. Some businesses will have to get fucked up first THEN they can start hiring humans again

62194083
>>62188643
this is serious

>>62188963
you'll be poorer than you ever imagined soon

>>62190529
this. I would like to subscribe thank you

62194913
>>62194027
even tho the faggot you replied to was replying to me, I believe his story. I joined a contractor IT job and they had many clients whom I got to interact with. Every single one of them liked me and boasted about the work i did. I reached out to the one i liked most when I was ready to rejoin employment after a year break and they swooped me up with no interview or credential checking. I do not have any certs, dropped out of college since it was a meme and a scam and learned everything through projects I was doing based from my hobbies (gaming).
>certs, degrees, etc are useless in clown world
>proof of work always works, like BTC if your proof of work is amazing YOU WILL BE SOUGHT OUT
>after a year of interacting with AIs, i asked them to give me an IQ estimate based upon our interactions and all of them told me 135+
While you are worrying about wasting time "meeting" their requirements for work, employers are wasting time meeting my requirements to work for them. You and I are not the same

62197152
>>62184413
Try saying that in public to 1/4 of the population and see what happens.

62200034
>>62184286
there's a looooot of boomers still in the workforce, and the news now says they have more wealth than all other generations combined.

62200057
>>62185888
I decided I wanted to go to law school and then was brutally denied with a 167 while negresses who complied they only had a 151 were accepted. Not to good schools, mind you, but seeing some 30yo fat black chick making 20k a month in DC and complaining she can't make ends meet and finding out she only got a 155 on the LSAT but went to Harvard Law is just a massive FUCK you.

62200063
>>62186171
ironic since Shrub is the reason things got so bad.

>>62186790
Capitalism and eugenics are inherently at odds, social darwinism means I'm smart enough to bash you in with a rock while you cry as I execute your kids gang style. Capitalism is your daddy got you a nice job and you think you're a tough guy. These are at opposite ends of the value spectrum.

62200068
>>62194913
networking doesn't matter when you get your head bashed in with a rock.

62200076
>>62200034
there's a 75yo at the local mail room who glares at me when I come in, but is like sugar for every 20-30yo woman. He's a lazy fuck and a creep. These guys are one bad day from keeling over and they're still competing with us for women, our fathers/grandfathers, like it's 1975. I hate them so bad.

62200088
>>62200057
Fuck, really? What was your GPA?

62200155
>>62197152
Most people have a job NEETcel.

62200169
>>62200155
You do know that they've been consistently revising those numbers way down right? I have a job currently myself, but I know Jerome Powell himself was admitting that the job market is probably a lot worse than what it has looked like on the surface in the last couple years.

62200185
>>62184286
>Every job is fake, they will interview for 3 or 4 rounds and still repost the job next week, after ghosting people of course
I've been saying this before but this is the biggest class action law suit in American history waiting to happen. the sheer premeditated and coldhearted and quite honestly treasonous fraud perpetrated on the American labor force is monstrous in size and weight. the amount of time, energy, labor, and national resources wasted by corporate America is of such magnitude it's almost incomprehensible yet it still pales in comparison to the psychological and emotional damages and torture caused by callous greedy corporate monsters to the average American. it's not exaggeration to call the actions of corporations writing out fake job positions never to be filled as plainly one thing, and one thing only: job terrorism. the impact is not imagined, it is too real: innocent, hardworking Americans armed with nothing but a dream of a better life and the pursuit of happiness have committed suicide because of the irresponsible behavior of corporations. corporate America is guilty, guilty of treason, guilty of murder, guilty of the most heinous crimes in American history: any American citizen who has ever been invited to a ghost job interview is according to my estimations owed anywhere between $1-5 million dollars in damages paid. I'm not a lawyer nor is this financial advice nor should anything I write or say ever be taken seriously especially not in this ironic anime board but all I have to say is God Bless America and damn those evil ghost jobs to hell!

62200599
>>62185125
>>62185702
>>62186168
>>62190507
Trump did that

62204337
>>62200068
sure it does, if you networked properly that person wouldnt be bashing your head in. Sad to see you think networking only is one thing, when its a pretty big concept

62207944
>got rejected from
>KFC:
>Maccas
>5 stocking jobs
>Burger King
>14(!) service jobs
>3 dishwasher jobs
>2 warehouse jobs
>4 healthcare jobs
>2 delivering jobs
WHERE ARE THE FUCKING JOBS I LIVE IN THE NETHERLANDS THERE SHOULD BE JOBS

62208527
>>62207944
What kind of druggie felony history do you have to fail to get any of those? Have you tried signing up for a temp agency?

62209302
>>62184335
Unironically the 3rd choice.

62209356
I’m gay

62209564
>>62207944
All the jobs are taken by illegal immigrants desu. Not enough has been deported. Would be nice if trump tripled the deportation rate.
At least its not your situation and they are actively letting them in.

62211206
>>62184335
I got hired to run the whole IT department, I will be the guy who sets up local AI LLMs in the business to replace employee roles. AMA

62212379
>>62184286
Fake job listings are so fucked up, it should really be at least a little bit illegal with how much they waste time, resources and sanity. Genuinely, creating fake job listings that require a lot of effort and lead nowhere is something an enemy nation would do as part of hybrid warfare, it's so effective at wasting the resources of productive members of society and making them demoralized and non-trusting of institutions.

item.title: ARE YOU FUCKING KIDDING ME WITH THIS SHIT STOP SELLING HANTA IS A NOTHING BURGER 40% MORTALITY JUST MEANS THEY BURN OUT… — 74 replies — 6.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
ARE YOU FUCKING KIDDING ME WITH THIS SHIT STOP SELLING HANTA IS A NOTHING BURGER 40% MORTALITY JUST MEANS THEY BURN OUT…

Open thread • Replies: 74 • Updated: 2026-05-10 17:38 UTC

OP

ARE YOU FUCKING KIDDING ME WITH THIS SHIT
STOP SELLING
HANTA IS A NOTHING BURGER
40% MORTALITY JUST MEANS THEY BURN OUT BEFORE SPREADING FUCK
I LITERALLY JUST BOUGHT BACK

Early replies (first 3)

62202000
Fuck off retard

62202033
>>62201978
>“it is possible more cases will be reported” because it can be up to six weeks before those infected with the Andes variant of hantavirus show symptoms.

Lmao 40% mortality with SIX WEEKS to transmit it would fucking DESTROY humanity if it's even mildly transmissible without symptoms.

62202089
>>62202033
Bullish on Hanta? Truly a nothing burger though, this disease is likely less transmissible than herpes.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62204874
Whats with the nonstop pandemics? The last one was just 6 years ago. Why we getting literal viral plague now?

62204956
>>62204672
there's no rule that they have to do the same thing twice. It could be different.

62204967
>>62204874
Israel's pr department are desperate to change the topic

62204988
>>62204967
I suppose this is it. Another nothing burger vax forced upon me

62205070
>>62202033
>if it's even mildly transmissible without symptoms.
It's not transmissible while asymptomatic, it's extremely hard to catch it while symptomatic (onset is like the flu so it can happen, but requires a lot of fluids and isn't aerosolized).

If you actually want to doomsay then the real issues is if it gets into rodent populations then it's just a recurring issue.

62205228
>>62205070
Then kill the rodents whats the big deal?

62205278
Stop falling for /pol/ bot threads. No one else cares about it at all

62205340
>>62202909
So like a one week timeframe they changed to possible human transmission? Just like this thing lol

62205341
>>62205278
Good point desu

62205352
humanity exists for 300000 years and now all of a sudden we have global humanity ending pandemics back to back within just a few years. yeah this seems a LITTLE bit unlikely and no I wont stop noticing.

62205358
>>62205340
Correct, they only have so much information so "the story" will change the moment they get more.

There's nothing wrong with that besides being a retard who thinks that since something is "the facts" all other possibilities have been "deboonked".

If you have an IQ over 110 you should be able to see the situation as one of many possibilities of varying probabilities, and update your weightings as more information becomes available.

Again, highly likely it won't transmit well enough but keep your eyes peeled for new cases.

62206237
>>62204470
We've known about corona viruses for a long ass time too. There's only been so many cases of this strain ever documented

62206252
>>62205352
Covid and now this banter plague would barely register in history; big nothingburgers. Mass media and the feverish obsession with keeping boomers alive as long as possible is what made covid register as a black-death tier plague in our (well, just normies') minds.

62206383
>>62206252
Just looked up the numbers again to rage-bait myself:

>According to provisional CDC mortality data, COVID‑19 death rates per 100,000 population rise steeply with age, from under1 in young children to over 1,395 per100,000 in those aged 85+ in 2021.

Imagine you're in Europe or China in 300ad, or the middle ages, and suddenly some unknown fever goes around. Out of everyone that contracts this mysterious fever, not even 0.1% dies. Literally NO ONE would care, and rightly so. The Justinian plague had a survival rate of less than 50%, now that is a pandemic to write home about.

62206439
>>62202089
It's a nothing burger to prepare you for a somethingburger

How would humanity react if they found out about sars? Mpox? Ebola?

Write down the findings and data and then plan your depopulation event accordingly.

One thing I've noticed on goytok is that normies are showing high levels of apathy and vitriol towards the current hanta passengers. This is likely to prepare them mentally to accept the coming deaths of the future.

tl:dr when cows are led to the slaughter they might freak out and go crazy. Imagine leading billions of cows and you're in a time crunch.

62206650
>>62206383
I wish it was the boomer destroyer they hyped it up to be.

62206967
>>62206237
And this is one of the documented and well-studied strains of hanta. Do try to keep up.

62208344
>>62202144
calls or puts

62208733
>>62202144
Unironically how do you make money from this?

62209911
>>62202190
Bill gates has modified it, made it easier to spread

62211386
>>62209911
omg

62211445
>Want a Hanta... Don't you want a...

62211652
>>62201978
if its another fauci shoah then I'm going to short the shit outta oil although im sure it'll be a short lived disease
>>62202909
"no significant human to human transmission"
this is like saying a 20% chance of death is the same as 0%
Hantavirus is a nothing burger if it started legitimately since its supposed to be a 0 chance of transmission and cruise ships are floating motel shitholes where they only clean things when needed and rats have an easy time living there

62211684
Lmao jews are right, goy cattle believes anything you tell them

62211771
>>62202796
Pfizer is a safe bet

62212348
>>62204346
This is like the 20th possible pandemic in 15 years. Remember SARS? Remember Avian flu? Remember Ebola in like 2017?
Nothing ever happens. Even Covid was a nothing burger.

62212356
>>62208733
Spy puts, puts on airlines, calls on Peloton

62212420
>>62201978
>he hasn't bought any MRNA
>he hasn't bought any NVAX
>he hasn't bought any PFE
Before any of you retards start screaming "nothingburger!!", understand that there have been plenty of nothingburger epidemics and these stocks move anyway.
The virus being a nothingburger doesn't mean the media won't spin it up for the tranny left to panic over. Don't miss out.

62212671
>>62202033
how many virologists would a superinteligent AI need to blackmail again??? at least 20 right?

item.title: >It doesn't matter what the average or median income is, it's all about where you live & how smart you are with money. — 41 replies — 4.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
>It doesn't matter what the average or median income is, it's all about where you live & how smart you are with money.

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-05-10 18:52 UTC

OP

>It doesn't matter what the average or median income is, it's all about where you live & how smart you are with money. If you make 65k in LA, NYC, Chicago, etc. you're broke as fuck while someone making 35k in a small midwestern town is living comfortably. You also wouldn't have to deal with drugs, crime, diversity, homeless, etc. So yeah, I'll take option 2 all day.

Why do rural suburban losers always say this? It is like saying you couldn’t make it in a nice area and now you have to move to a crappier area.

If that were true, why do wealthy people concentrate together in certain areas?

Early replies (first 3)

62208127
successful people get jobs in rich areas, I heard.
even crazier, those jobs pay well. I know right?

62208139
>>62208127
What are you saying?
We are talking about the guy who made the quote and whether his opinion is retarded or not.

62208178
>>62208139
nobody who lives in LA, NYC, or Chicago makes $65K, everyone who went to college makes at least $110-$150K unless that quote was from 1980 or something. this thread is gay. sage.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62208644
>>62208508
This is Meta strategy I plan on following.

>Live in NYC area. Right now, w/ parents. Stacking cash & investing.
>Looking to retire in less than 15 years (around 35-40).
>Move to beautiful low-cost area. Maybe Idaho. Maybe Japan.

62208687
>>62208595
Sure sure, the places filled with big homes and families living what gets constantly shilled online as an unobtainable dream... THAT'S the place for losers.

62208731
>>62208687
What?
The unobtainable dream is a house in the city but with lots of space and away from noise and riff raff.

62208735
>>62208687
What the fuck are you talking about?
People that cannot afford good areas move away from cities. The farther away the cheaper the properties.

62208751
>>62208100
>looks like a motivational thread
>peek inside
>demotivational

I'm here to tell you all, you can make it. You can be happy. You can retire early. You will find love. You will hang in there.

As long as you're not Indian lol theyre fucked forever.

62208846
>>62208751
Unfortunately, the average person is falling behind.

62208878
>>62208735
Those cheap properties far from cities have some areas with pretty damn big houses surrounded by pretty damn good nature and pretty damn awesome communities. But you'd rather be a pet for the 1% than consider improving your life because you've internalized some meme that your pod city life is high status.

Nice samefagging btw. If the average person is falling behind consider what dumb herd behaviors they're falling into and then break yourself free from it.

62209009
>>62208100
>You also wouldn't have to deal with drugs, crime, diversity, homeless, etc.
lol

62209014
>>62208878
High status is “pretty damn big houses surrounded by pretty damn good nature and pretty damn awesome communities” INSIDE the city. You fucking ruralfag.

62209025
> Those cheap properties far from cities have some areas with pretty damn big houses surrounded by pretty damn good nature and pretty damn awesome communities.
Location location location.

62209026
>>62208878
> If the average person is falling behind consider what dumb herd behaviors they're falling into and then break yourself free from it.
Why are you getting personal with me? I live in one of the areas I am describing. lol.

62209898
>>62208100
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62210093
>>62208508
This is it for me...and I'm taking it one step further by building my own house when I quit working. I will do 100% of the work myself. I am qualified, and taking 2 years and 200k or even less of my own money to build a non piece of shit house sounds better than taking a 500k mortgage on a money pit I additionally have to insure. Sand, rocks, and cement are cheaper than lumber if you do it yourself. My copper roof will scrap for more than I paid for it adjusted for inflation in 100 years when my great great grandchildren finally have to do the first roof maintenance.

62210276
>>62208512
>agender
lol

62210310
Inbred rural rednecks are ok with destroying the world because they only make 35k a year in el paso or something.

62210340
>>62208100
High end goods manufactured by multinational corporations don't get cheaper just because you live in rural Illinois or Estonia.
Cars
Phones
Electronics and appliances
All cost the same or even more.

So rest assured that the couple making $400k a year in suburban America has vastly more purchasing power than the couple making $100k in Estonia.

62210482
>>62208100
The world is changing rapidly. Things are more connected and globalized than ever before. Niggers in buttfuck nowhere south Asia have smart phones, tablets and good enough internet to run global businesses. They are a part of the same economy as some nigger in Idaho somehow. All global systems are catching up to this fact and trying to use it to their advantage and enslave you. Now is the time to think and do something else other than live in faggot ass Kentucky or try to pretend like you're someone important in a large city. Big cities around the world are being flooded with Americans that know this. You have think outside the box or be enslaved. Create your own reality and be sovereign or be a golem in someone else's.

TLDR Reject false dichotomy from 4chan

62210530
None of this matters if you're lonely.

62210590
>>62208100
>nice area
The elderly mother of the grinning TV with lived in an "affluent area" in arizona which is full of spics and next to mexico and she was abducted from her home and executed in mexico. There are no such things are nice or safe areas when nonwhites live there.

62210931
>>62210590
So much cope from poor people.

62210982
>>62208644
It’s meta to miss out on your 20s to maybe retire at 40?

62210999
>>62210982
Wtf is 20’s?
Graduate uni by 25 and enter full time work?

62211220
>>62210999
He’s talking about living with his parents in his 20s

62211970
>>62208100
You have a higher quality of life if you can get a remote job that pays moderately well, and then move to a poorfag nation where the currency you are paid in is worth heaps more than the local one.

62211990
>>62208100
Don't assume grain farmers are only making $35k a year. Most are actually millionaires. And most rural people I know have multiple streams on income, and cohabitate with family members, on land they own.

62211993
>>62208100
There are no “nice areas” in big cities because they are full of third world scum. American cities are disgusting shit holes like any other third world shit hole.

62212005
>>62208100
> rural suburban losers always say this
Because it’s worth it to make less, own more, and not be forced to live around urban goy cattle strivers.

62212279
>>62211993
Well he's got you there Mutts.

62212605
>>62208100
>Why do rural suburban losers always say this? It is like saying you couldn’t make it in a nice area and now you have to move to a crappier area.

i make 115k a year in texas and i can apply to jobs with the same qualifications that pay like 200k a year in california and i'd still be poorer all things considered

62212894
>>62212605
My jewish sister in law makes $225k plus bonuses remote in Dallas.

item.title: >redditors are getting rich and posting their 1M dollar screenshots from investing in RKLB and AMD >everyone here is poo… — 18 replies — 3.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
>redditors are getting rich and posting their 1M dollar screenshots from investing in RKLB and AMD >everyone here is poo…

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-10 22:25 UTC

OP

>redditors are getting rich and posting their 1M dollar screenshots from investing in RKLB and AMD
>everyone here is poor as fuck

Early replies (first 3)

62208450
just goes to show 4chan is much more retarded than >leddit :^)

62208458
only a few more weeks until I can post on reddit

62208475
>>62208412
We were targeted specifically by kikes to be made poor

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62208495
>>62208412
i've been pointing out how reddit outperforms /biz/ for years and nothing has changed here

62208497
>>62208495
How would you even begin to prove such a baseless assertion? You think redditors invented dollar cost averaging, portfolio diversity, or having optimism about tech industries?

62208501
>>62208497
no way to prove it, i have simply observed the average frequency of gains and reddit is the clear winner

/biz/ however won with the OG LINK marines

62208528
>>62208412
>>everyone here is poor as fuck
Everyone here is the most retarded kind of skeptic, bagholder fudding new investments, or bear who thinks the market is going back to 30k on the DJIA for nk reason.

62208530
>>62208501
>>62208497
>>62208475
Reddit doesn't ruthlessly purge The Next Big thing until it's too late to make sizable gains, it doesn't have low IQ browns phoneposting "SAAR BUY THE TOP OF A MASSIVE CANDLE" it doesn't have 50yo xoomers fudding every good investment becuase "muh tech bubble was bad" 20 years ago.

62208534
>>62208528
kek you are poor

62208537
>>62208458
my ip is range banned from reddit permanently

62208538
Don't kid yourselves. Crypto is in the beginnings of a decade long bear market. /pmg/fags will get the final laugh.

62208544
>>62208528
you keep taking my screenshot. I want to strangle you

62208568
>>62208530
Reddit does, in fact, purge a lot of its comments. It is also filled to the brim with botposting. Don't know why you'd treat their circlejerk as more valuable than any other random shitpost you read online.

>>62208544
I should take it and use it as mine too.

62208833
>>62208412
I see WSB getting filthy rich meanwhile /biz/ chuds are here bagholding chainlink and "investing" in rocks

62209921
>>62208412
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62209936
>>62208501
the weird part here is we have bears that never post ANY gains LOL

62211249
>>62208537
based
>>62209921
go post that over there

62213463
>>62208412
I'm doing ok

item.title: Icy peepeepoopoo sisters... we're mooning. Are we finally going to 2k EOY? — 22 replies — 2.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Icy peepeepoopoo sisters... we're mooning. Are we finally going to 2k EOY?

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-05-10 19:59 UTC

OP

Icy peepeepoopoo sisters... we're mooning. Are we finally going to 2k EOY?

Early replies (first 3)

62208172
>>62208149
/pol/ brainlets btfo, ok? Moonman scam finally getting reveal

62208270
>>62208149
What’s your dca, mine is $7 and I am still Fkn rekted hard

62208363
>>62208270
Like $4.5 kek but it doesn't matter. Wagmi pee chads. Moonman was right and we're going to 2k simple as.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62208375
>>62208363
That’s really good, I stopped buying long ago because became super demoralized. I still am I believe this pump will fail like the others

62208404
>>62208149
Keep am eye on it.
Some guy on here said the same about some other crypto, it doubled in a week but that was it.

62208433
>>62208404
Cope. ICP 2k EOY simple as. Heil Moonman 1488 WAGMI

62208498
Hmm,
now i just need to remember how to get into my wallet.
the internet identity thing is retarded

62208514
Check my digits newfag niggers >>62208433
Icy piss 2k per token EOY faggots
RWDS are coming for the piss fudders fr fr no cap bussin not sussin

62208516
Jeeezus jim, 45k? Did you used to have 500k or something?

62208518
>>62208363
>moonman
Niggas formed a cult around a tripfag who stopped posting 4 years ago.

62208522
>>62208498
Are you legitimately retarded?
You do know that you can now login to your internet identity through shit like google accounts, right

62208809
>>62208518
Yeah and? Moonman is based

62209068
>>62208149
When i see this kind of shit pumping wo the big ones, i know its just another alt exit pump..... Sell everything!

62209173
it makes sense, many computers are on the internet these days

62209181
ICP chuds are somehow an even more tragic group than stink marines.

They achieved the impossible.

62209465
How can i sell my 8 year locked neuron?

62210651
>>62209465
Surely you mean 2 year locked neuron

62211255
>>62208149
holy fuck.

62211257
>>62208270
I'm just under $5, 5k bottles..
>>62208363
I believe. Btw I'm cashing out a milly at $1k though

62211860
>>62208433
Were back bros

62212936
>>62208433
It’s inevitable

62213071
>>62211257
i boughteded more yesterday, 6k now.

item.title: Self-employed 401(k) - Full control — 17 replies — 2.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Self-employed 401(k) - Full control

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-05-10 21:46 UTC

OP

Anyone here ever take advantage of a self-employed 401(k)? Apparently you can rollover an old employer's funds straight into a self-employed 401(k) and gain total control. As we all know, these employer 401k account have pretty terrible returns. It'd be nice to at least throw it into something like VOO or even YOLO into some individual stocks. Kind of surprised I have never heard of you brokie ass chuds talk about it before. I already own a small business, so I have that part taken care of. Seems like a great way to grow wealth much more quickly. Imagine sinking your entire 401k into Micron kek.

Early replies (first 3)

62211140
>>62211117
You can also just transfer it into an ordinary 401k rollover account at your favorite brokerage and get total control that way, no need to be self employed. I've got two 401k's rolled into my Schwab rollover account now, and am about to transfer another in a week or so (employer got bought and the 401k didn't automatically transfer over to the new plan).

62211147
>>62211117
Scams

62211165
>>62211117
yeah i did that and bought bitcoin and asian ETFs with my 401k

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62211175
>>62211140
The problem is I have a traditional IRA account that I use explicitly for backdoor roth IRA (because I make so much money). I have to keep 401k funds out of there or it messes up the backdoor roth strategy.

62211184
>>62211175
So create a separate rollover account for your 401k's. Use a different broker if it makes you feel better.

62211215
>>62211184
It doesn't matter in the eyes of the IRS. All traditional IRAs are considered one regardless of how many accounts you have.

62211254
>>62211117
you will be destroyed in a bear market

62211277
>>62211117
isn't this just an IRA?

62211281
>broke ass chuds
Tiresome scam thread by retard from twitter whose 'business' is either landscaping, selling some app no one needs or scamming other idiotso n twitter.

62211296
>>62211277
No an self-employed 401k is legally different. Look it up on fidelity

62211300
>>62211281
I am a retard on twitter, but my small business is game development. I have 3 games on Steam.

62211310
>>62211277
They are similar but with some differences. I already have a traditional ira that serves as a staging ground for doing backdoor roth transfers.

62212768
>>62211117
Employee sponsord 401(k) - you work for someone else. If do they match? Find out what is the most you can contribute.

You own your own business - employer (you) sponsored 401(K) you, I believe, can set up the employer 401k and have the employer (you) make contributions for you (as employee), and can set, to some degree, the amount you can contribute. In this 401k you can set up your own self dircted retirement brokerage account and accumlate tax free.

Also look into SEPs

If you have a trusted partner, you might create a "non-profit" and donate enough each year to the non-profit or charity to hold the money (look up tax benefits of donating to those that you own/control and the very limited amount of money you actually have to spend to meet the stated objectives of the non profit or charity (these were created by the wealthy for a reason), make tax free investments on your behalf. Aslo with governments or some non-profits (I believe) you can set a 457(b) plan with no minimum age for penalty free distriubtions so you can retire at say 40 and pull from you 457 (the 401k requires you to be 59-1/2.

Talk to a an attorney who is very familiar with doing these things, the contribution limits, tax benefits and so on to see if you can make a plan. With more money you can hir the right people to help you develop ways to save more money.

62212901
>>62211140
Yea I have all my employer 401ks rolled into one self directed IRA. Like, wow so many different employers.

62212930
>>62211117
You should roll it over to an ira unless you need to avoid th pro rata rule or want to use the rule of 55...maaaaybe a few other edge cases like 401k loans as well.

62212997
>>62211117
Isn't that like a CD account but they promise you 1% higher interest but then take money if you want to cash out?

62213360
>p-please bail out my boomer obligations
Nope. Go belly up pension funds. Its irrational to buy out a demographic overhang

item.title: make it — 8 replies — 2.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
make it

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-05-11 00:50 UTC

OP

I have 1.6 BTC and $70K in liquid cash. Am I gonna make it?

Early replies (first 3)

62213781
I doubt it.

62213782
You can make it, OP.

62213794
>>62213782
I am also a right wing extremist. Does this help or hurt my 'folio?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62213809
>>62213794
As long as the jews don’t know about it

62213815
>>62213794
As long as you don't fall into the same trap as the rainbow retard lefty brigade and make all your investments ideological.

62213816
>>62213778
how old are you?

62213832
>>62213816
28

62213835
No.

item.title: >Historical analysis and economic theories suggest that beard prevalence often increases during periods of economic coll… — 8 replies — 1.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
>Historical analysis and economic theories suggest that beard prevalence often increases during periods of economic coll…

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-05-11 00:07 UTC

OP

>Historical analysis and economic theories suggest that beard prevalence often increases during periods of economic collapse or recession. This phenomenon is attributed to several key factors: Signaling Masculinity: Research indicates that during times of high unemployment and economic hardship, men may grow beards to compensate for a perceived loss of status or professional identity, emphasizing other aspects of masculinity when traditional markers like career success are unavailable.

>Freedom from Corporate Norms:
>Unemployment removes the strict grooming codes often enforced in corporate environments. The
"recession beard" became a notable trend during the 2007-2009 financial crisis, as laid-off workers gained the freedom to experiment with their appearance without workplace stigma. Cyclical Fashion Trends: The concept of "peak beard" theorizes that facial hair follows a roughly 30-year cycle driven by sexual selection and scarcity. Economic downturns, such as the 2008 financial crisis and the Great Depression, have historically coincided with surges in beard popularity, similar to patterns observed after the Wall Street Crash of the 1920s.

>Anti-Establishment Symbolism: Historically, beards have also served as a marker of rebellion or anti-capitalist sentiment. During periods of economic instability, facial hair can signal a rejection of traditional corporate conformity, a trend notably amplified by the rise of the tech industry and Silicon Valley culture in the 21st century.
>Now you can add beards - at least for some men. The rise of the "recession beard" has drawn commentary in some quarters

>The consensus opinion seems to be that unemployment offers the beard grower a greater level of freedom to experiment with his appearance .


TL:DR beards = low status male

Early replies (first 3)

62213242
Adults wear flip flops and Super Mario shirts to their jobs at corporate bank offices now. There are plenty of low status people.
Beards are not necessarily low status though. If you put on a suit then people perceive you as having more authority. Clean shaven people are associated with middle management. Only C-suite tend to pulp of the beard. It's a bit of a horseshoe chart.
>One of the most common associations with beards is masculinity. Across cultures, facial hair has long been linked to strength, maturity, and confidence. Psychological studies suggest that men with beards are often perceived as more authoritative and capable leaders.
But most of you dysgenic mutants will never pull that off and having a beard or not doesn't really matter for you.

62213308
Men with beards are seen as more trustworthy and honest in a business setting.

62213309
>>62213242
Beards = low status

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62213328
>>62213134
"Corporate norms" aren't a thing anymore anon. The weird IT people are wearing anime shirts and jean shorts and nobody can tell Shaquanda the diversity hire she can't wear sweatpants and have four inch nails.

62213337
>>62213134
>>62213309
Faggot can't grow a beard. Ive had so many women touch my beard just to feel it. I lead men and work in an industry with very few women. I have four children. Its good to be a good man.

62213722
>>62213337
Youre a loser.
+ low status.

62213725
>>62213337
You’re low status, bro. Trust me.
Enjoy the fecal matter in your beard.
>T. retired at 24.

62213750
>>62213337
The only thing you lead are the soi bois in your soi latte enjoyer reddit group

item.title: XMR — 4 replies — 1.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
XMR

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-05-11 02:05 UTC

OP

Delisted at 160, currently at 410

Early replies (first 3)

62213668
Nobody gives two shits about crapto in general. You are a baggie trying to find a greater fool, you were the greatest fool

62213696
>>62213668
You are cattle.

62213702
>>62213696
No, I am cashflow that is never going to have a demand for your bags. No matter what you baggies advertise

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62214009
>>62213668
I made it through XMR, it's an excellent opportunity for anyone who listens.
